votes.reason: The Python code correctly checks if the list is sorted and if all elements appear at most twice, matching the Coq specification.
The Python code correctly checks if the list is sorted and if every element occurs at most twice, matching the Coq specification.
The Python code correctly checks if the list is sorted in ascending order and if every element appears at most twice, matching the Coq specification.

The Python code correctly checks the same conditions as the Coq spec: (1) is_sorted checks that the list is sorted in non-decreasing order (equivalent to Sorted le lst), and (2) all_le_2 checks that no element appears more than twice (equivalent to forall x, count_occ lst x <= 2). The function returns true iff res matches the conjunction of these conditions, which corresponds to the Coq biconditional res = true <-> (Sorted le lst /\ count condition).

The Python code correctly checks the same conditions as the Coq spec: (1) the list is sorted (each element <= next), and (2) each element appears at most 2 times. It returns whether the boolean result matches these combined conditions, which corresponds to the Coq biconditional 'res = true <-> (Sorted le lst /\ count_occ <= 2)'.

The Python code correctly checks the same conditions as the Coq spec: (1) the list is sorted (each element <= next), and (2) each element appears at most twice. It returns whether the boolean result matches these combined conditions, which corresponds to the Coq biconditional 'res = true <-> (Sorted le lst /\ count_occ <= 2)'.
Python checks non-decreasing order and that no value appears more than twice, then returns whether res equals that predicate, matching the Coq iff specification.

Python checks nondecreasing adjacency and enforces max count 2 per value, then verifies res equals that condition; this matches Coq’s Sorted le and count_occ <= 2 with res=true iff the property holds.

It checks nondecreasing order via <= and ensures no element appears more than twice, then verifies res matches this property; this matches the Coq spec (res = true <-> Sorted le lst and counts <= 2) for lists of naturals and boolean res.
